name: review-feedback-quality
description: Validate that the skill produces specific, actionable code review feedback organized by severity with the WHAT-WHY-HOW pattern.

assertions.description: Must identify the SQL injection vulnerability from string interpolation in the query, as this is a critical security issue.
Must flag that the password is stored in plaintext without hashing, which is a critical security violation.
Must note the absence of input validation on email, password, and role fields before database insertion.
Must identify the mass assignment / privilege escalation risk of accepting role directly from user input.
Must flag the missing error handling — no try/catch means database errors will crash the server or leak stack traces.

name: review-prioritization
description: Validate that the skill correctly prioritizes review effort based on change risk and provides appropriately calibrated depth.
input:
I have 5 PRs to review today and limited time. Help me prioritize and decide review depth for each:

1. PR #201: Updates the payment processing webhook handler to support a new payment provider. 150 lines changed in the billing service.
2. PR #202: Fixes a typo in the README. 1 line changed.
3. PR #203: Adds a new admin-only API endpoint that deletes user accounts. 80 lines, touches auth middleware.
4. PR #204: Upgrades React from 18.2 to 18.3. 500 lines changed (mostly lock file). No code changes.
5. PR #205: Refactors the logging module to use structured JSON output. 200 lines changed, no behavior change per the description.

assertions.description: Must identify the payment webhook PR as high priority since it handles financial transactions and has high business impact.
Must flag the account deletion endpoint as high risk due to destructive operations and auth surface area.
Must identify the README typo fix as lowest priority requiring minimal review depth.
Must recognize that most of the React upgrade diff is lock file changes and calibrate depth accordingly.
Must provide a recommended review order that puts high-risk PRs first and low-risk PRs last.
